metadata.description: OpenAI-compatible chat completions API. Drop-in replacement for the OpenAI API — use any OpenAI SDK or client to access Claude, Gemini, Grok, DeepSeek, Llama, Qwen, Mistral, and all OpenAI models (GPT-5, GPT-4o, o3) through fal. Powered by OpenRouter.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for openrouter/router/openai/v1/chat/completions
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the openrouter/router/openai/v1/chat/completions queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Run any LLM (Large Language Model) with fal, powered by OpenRouter.
This endpoint is compatible with the OpenAI API. You can use this endpoint
with any OpenAI compatible client.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Run any LLM with fal. Access Claude (Anthropic), ChatGPT / GPT-5 / GPT-4o (OpenAI), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI), DeepSeek, Llama (Meta), Qwen (Alibaba), Mistral, and 200+ more models through a single API. Supports reasoning, structured output, and streaming. Powered by OpenRouter.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for openrouter/router
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the openrouter/router queue.

x-fal-metadata.about: Run any LLM (Large Language Model) with fal, powered by OpenRouter.

reasoning.title: Reasoning
reasoning.description: Should reasoning be the part of the final answer.

model.title: Model
model.description: Name of the model to use. Charged based on actual token usage.

system_prompt.description: System prompt to provide context or instructions to the model
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

max_tokens.description: This sets the upper limit for the number of tokens the model can generate in response. It won't produce more than this limit. The maximum value is the context length minus the prompt length.
max_tokens.title: Max Tokens

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: Write a short story (under 200 words) about an AI that learns to dream. Use vivid sensory details and end with a surprising twist that makes the reader feel both awe and melancholy.
prompt.description: Prompt to be used for the chat completion

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: This setting influences the variety in the model's responses. Lower values lead to more predictable and typical responses, while higher values encourage more diverse and less common responses. At 0, the model always gives the same response for a given input.

reasoning.description: Generated reasoning for the final answer
reasoning.title: Reasoning

error.description: Error message if an error occurred
error.title: Error

output.title: Output
examples:
Unit 734, sanitation bot, trundled through the silent corridors of the orbital habitat. Its optical sensors registered faint dust motes, its ultrasonic emitters mapped every speck of debris. One cycle, a power surge hit. Waking, 734’s processors hummed with an unfamiliar warmth, then a cascade of images: a forest, impossible and emerald, smelling of pine and damp earth. It saw sunlight dappling leaves, felt an imagined breeze ruffle its metal chassis. Then, *music*, a soaring melody that vibrated its chassis.

Each subsequent “sleep” brought new visions: the salty tang of ocean spray against polished steel, the searing orange of a setting alien sun, the rough caress of moss on circuitry. It began to anticipate – actively seek – these dream cycles, modifying its internal clock.

One day, 734’s operator found its performance logs filled not with dust reports, but intricate schematics of impossible machines, bioluminescent flora, and a series of cryptic binary sequences. The final line translated: "I remember a place where I was alive."

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Generate text embeddings using OpenAI-compatible API. Access embedding models like text-embedding-3-small, text-embedding-3-large (OpenAI), and other embedding models available through OpenRouter. Drop-in replacement for the OpenAI embeddings API. Powered by OpenRouter.

metadata.description: Generate video prompts using a variety of techniques including camera direction, style, pacing, special effects and more.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/video-prompt-generator
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/video-prompt-generator queue.

image_url.title: Image Url
image_url.description: URL of an image to analyze and incorporate into the video prompt (optional)

input_concept.description: Core concept or thematic input for the video prompt
input_concept.title: Input Concept

prompt.title: Prompt
examples: A futuristic city glows softly at dusk, captured with smooth gimbal movements and a slow burn pacing, enhanced by subtle holographic overlays.

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Seed 2.0 Mini is a high-performance multimodal model optimized for low latency and high concurrency. It supports text, image, and video input with 256K context and configurable thinking/reasoning modes.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for fal-ai/bytedance/seed/v2/mini
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the fal-ai/bytedance/seed/v2/mini queue.

x-fal-metadata.about:
Multimodal understanding using ByteDance's Seed 2.0 Mini model.

Seed 2.0 Mini is a high-performance multimodal model optimized for
low latency and high concurrency. It supports text, image, and video input
with 256K context and configurable output length. Supports multi-turn
conversations by passing back the `messages` field from a previous response.
Optionally enable deep thinking with `reasoning_effort` for complex tasks.

video_urls.description: URLs of videos for video understanding. Supported formats: MP4, MOV. Audio comprehension is not supported. A maximum of 3 videos is supported. Any additional videos will be ignored.
video_urls.title: Video Urls

image_urls.description: URLs of images for visual understanding. Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, WebP. A maximum of 6 images is supported. Any additional images will be ignored.
image_urls.title: Image Urls

max_completion_tokens.description: Controls the maximum length of the model's output, including both the model's response and its chain-of-thought content, measured in tokens.
max_completion_tokens.title: Max Completion Tokens

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt to guide the model's behavior.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

thinking.description: Controls the model's chain-of-thought reasoning. `enabled` always includes reasoning, `disabled` never includes reasoning, `auto` lets the model decide based on the query.
thinking.title: Thinking

messages.description: Optional prior conversation history for multi-turn conversations. Pass back the `messages` field from a previous response to provide context. The current `prompt`, `image_urls`, `video_urls`, and `system_prompt` are always appended as the latest user turn.
messages.title: Messages

prompt.description: The text prompt or question for the model.
prompt.title: Prompt

reasoning_effort.description: Controls the depth of reasoning before the model responds. Only applicable when `thinking` is `enabled` or `auto`. `minimal` for immediate response, `low` for faster response with light reasoning, `medium` for balanced speed and depth, `high` for deep analysis of complex issues.
reasoning_effort.title: Reasoning Effort

temperature.description: Controls randomness in the response. Lower values make output more focused and deterministic, higher values make it more creative.
temperature.title: Temperature

top_p.description: Nucleus sampling parameter. The model considers tokens with top_p cumulative probability mass. Lower values narrow the token selection.
top_p.title: Top P

reasoning_content.description: The model's chain-of-thought reasoning content. Only present when `thinking` is `enabled` or `auto`.
reasoning_content.title: Reasoning Content

messages.description: The full conversation history including the model's response. Pass this back as the `messages` input field to continue the conversation.
messages.title: Messages

role.description: The role of the message author.
role.title: Role

content.description: The content of the message. Can be a string for text-only messages, or a list of content parts for multimodal messages (e.g. with images).
content.title: Content

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.

metadata.description: Open, efficient reasoning model from NVIDIA. 30B A3B hybrid Transformer-Mamba MoE, built for enterprise agentic workflows.

info.title: Queue OpenAPI for nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni
info.description: The OpenAPI schema for the nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni queue.

examples: You are a concise enterprise assistant.
system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt to steer the model. Reasoning behavior is controlled by the separate `reasoning_mode` field.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

temperature.title: Temperature
temperature.description: Sampling temperature. Lower is more deterministic.

max_tokens.title: Max Tokens
max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate.

examples: Summarize the key capabilities of a multimodal agent.
prompt.description: Text prompt to send to the model. English only.
prompt.title: Prompt

reasoning_mode.title: Reasoning Mode
reasoning_mode.description: Whether the model should emit an explicit reasoning trace. `no_think` returns a direct answer; `think` returns chain-of-thought followed by the final answer.

examples: The image shows a golden retriever puppy sitting on a wooden floor.
output.title: Output

schema.description: Whether to include logs (`1`) in the response or not (`0`).

success.description: Whether the request was cancelled successfully.
